item.author: noreply@rladies.org (R-Ladies St. Louis)

item.title: ChatGPT in RStudio with gptstudio: Better than GitHub Copilot? - R-Ladies St. Louis
item.description:
Chapter: R-Ladies St. Louis

When: Friday, March 27, 2026 at 3:00 PM UTC

Where: Online event

Attendees: 33 going

**Supercharging Your R Workflow with a Native AI Assistant** It’s no surprise that large language models like ChatGPT are helpful for coding. What makes gptstudio stand out is how it brings that help directly into RStudio through a clean, user-friendly add-in. gptstudio is an R package that integrates ChatGPT into RStudio, allowing you to generate code, explain functions, and write documentation using natural language, all without leaving your IDE. It supports various OpenAI models and offers an add-in panel that works like a built-in assistant. This tool is especially useful when you're experimenting, writing boilerplate code, or just need quick answers while staying in your flow. Advantages of using the gptstudio package: ✔️ Seamless integration into RStudio via an add-in panel ✔️ Helps generate and explain R code using plain language prompts ✔️ Supports multiple OpenAI models, including GPT-3.5 and GPT-4 ✔️ Useful for both beginners and experienced users looking to speed up their workflow

View event details
item.author: noreply@rladies.org (R-Ladies St. Louis)

item.title: Create Custom Webpages with R and Quarto - R-Ladies Vancouver
item.description:
Chapter: R-Ladies Vancouver

When: Wednesday, March 4, 2026 at 3:00 AM UTC

Where: Online event

Attendees: 16 going

**Workshop Details** In this workshop, we will learn how to create webpages using Quarto in RStudio, and host it on GitHub. The example website will be a professional website template ([see my example webpage here](https://grcetmpk.github.io/)), but these skills can also be used to create other webpages ([like this one](https://ubc-stat.github.io/stat545/)). This workshop was designed for R coders with no prior experience with Quarto. Experience with GitHub is helpful, but not necessary. We will be going through all of the steps slowly. \*\*\* 1. If this is your first R-Ladies event, please take a moment to review our R-Ladies Global code of conduct: [https://github.com/rladies/starter-kit/wiki/Code-of-Conduct](https://github.com/rladies/starter-kit/wiki/Code-of-Conduct) 2. Who is this allowed to attend the event? Everyone is welcome to join us at our events and to be a part of our mission to empower gender minorities with the skills and knowledge to code in one of the top programming language in data science, which is R. 3\. To fulfill this mission we do ask participants to respect a focus on the encouragement and support for women and non\-binary people during our events\. See you there! Jasmine, and Melissa

View event details
item.author: noreply@rladies.org (R-Ladies Vancouver)

item.title: Coffee and Conversation - R-Ladies St. Louis
item.description:
Chapter: R-Ladies St. Louis

When: Saturday, February 28, 2026 at 4:30 PM UTC

Where: Webster Groves Public Library

Attendees: 16 going

Join us for a morning of conversation and networking with fellow women in data. If you’re interested in data careers, looking to hire from underrepresented groups in data, or wanting to brainstorm about a project you’ve been working on, we’d love to see you. This is an in-person event and refreshments will be provided, so we appreciate your RSVP.

View event details
item.author: noreply@rladies.org (R-Ladies St. Louis)

item.title: From Incident to Solution: How IT Functional Support Keeps the Business Running - R Ladies Tirane
item.description:
Chapter: R Ladies Tirane

When: Monday, February 23, 2026 at 5:00 PM UTC

Where: Online event

Attendees: 7 going

Open Lecture: **"From Incident to Solution: How IT Functional Support Keeps the Business Running"** Part of SPARK series IT incidents don’t just affect systems, they impact operations, safety, and business continuity. In this session, we’ll explore how IT Functional Application Support turns incidents into solutions and plays a key role in keeping maritime operations running smoothly. **🎤 Speaker: Rinalda Prifti** **Functional Applications Support, Smit Lamnalco Netherlands** 📅 Date: February 23, 2026 ⏰ Time: 18:00 📍 Format: Online (Zoom) 👉 Register on Meetup to receive the Zoom link and all the details.

View event details
item.author: noreply@rladies.org (R Ladies Tirane)

item.title: Wrangling Earth System Model Outputs in R for Marine Ecologists - R-Ladies Santa Barbara
item.description:
Chapter: R-Ladies Santa Barbara

When: Thursday, February 19, 2026 at 1:30 AM UTC

Where: Online event

Attendees: 18 going

R is undoubtedly the programming language of choice for many marine ecologists. However, navigating the complexities of downloading, processing and wrangling gridded oceanographic data within R can represent a steep learning curve. As the impacts of climate variability and change on our oceans become increasingly evident, the ability to efficiently interact with, and use, Earth System Model (ESM) outputs for our research is more critical than ever. Here, we will walk through core skills needed to be proficient in creating projections of physical variables from ESM outputs, with the goal of using them for marine ecological applications. Specifically, we’ll be creating projections of sea-surface temperature out to 2100, across one climate scenario, for the California Current region. This event will be held on **Wednesday, February 18 from 5:30 - 7:00 PST** and will be **virtual**! A zoom link and the workshop materials will be provided closer to the event to folks who RSVP. **RSVPs will close at noon the day of the event.**

View event details
item.author: noreply@rladies.org (R-Ladies Santa Barbara)

item.title: A gRadual Intro to Shiny - R-Ladies Remote
item.description:
Chapter: R-Ladies Remote

When: Tuesday, February 17, 2026 at 4:00 PM UTC

Where: Online event

Attendees: 55 going

Curious about Shiny, but not sure where to start? This hands-on workshop is designed to ease you in gradually. In A gRadual Intro to Shiny, Ted Laderas will walk through the basics of building interactive web applications in R using Shiny, with an emphasis on understanding concepts step by step rather than jumping straight into complex apps. We’ll start from simple ideas and build up carefully, so you can see how each piece fits together. This workshop is ideal for: \- R users who are new to Shiny \- People who have tried Shiny before but felt overwhelmed \- Anyone interested in turning analyses into interactive tools You’ll learn: \- What Shiny is and how it works \- How UI and server components fit together \- How reactivity works \(without the panic\) \- How to build and modify a simple Shiny app No prior Shiny experience is required, but some familiarity with R will be helpful. The session will include live demos, examples, and plenty of time to ask questions. Come for the code, stay for the “aha!” moments, and leave with a solid foundation for building your own Shiny apps.

View event details
item.author: noreply@rladies.org (R-Ladies Remote)

item.title: From Data to Decisions: Using Digital Twins for Smarter Maintenance - R-Ladies Rome
item.description:
Chapter: R-Ladies Rome

When: Monday, February 16, 2026 at 5:00 PM UTC

Where: Online event

Attendees: 53 going

**How do you turn raw sensor data into reliable maintenance decisions when systems operate under constantly changing conditions?** In this talk, [Priyanka Schnell](https://www.linkedin.com/in/priyanka-schnell/) will share a **real-world case study** from the aerospace industry, showing how data and engineering models can be used to move from traditional, schedule-based maintenance to smarter, condition-based decisions. The example comes from aircraft wing manufacturing, where robotic drills operate under extremely strict quality requirements. ***"Replacing tools too early wastes resources, while replacing them too late can cause costly damage."*** Priyanka will explain how combining sensor data (such as power, vibration, and torque) with physical knowledge of the process makes it possible to detect early warning signs of failure. Using a concept called a ***digital twin***—a live data model that mirrors the real process—the team was able to identify problems hours before failure occurred. This approach improved efficiency, reduced waste, and prevented expensive damage. To make the ideas concrete, parts of the session will include live exploration of data and models in **Google Colab**, focusing on intuition, **workflow**, and **decision-making** rather than heavy mathematics. The talk is designed to be accessible and will emphasize practical lessons, interpretability, and why combining data science with domain knowledge is key when working in production systems—no prior knowledge of manufacturing or physics required.

View event details
item.author: noreply@rladies.org (R-Ladies Rome)
